title: Tender Tandoor Irish Lamb Kebabs
description: Spicy, cheese-pull Irish lamb kebabs featuring perilla-leaf kkaennip, finished in a tandoor. Perfect for a date-night dinner.

ingredients.name: 1 pound Irish lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 cup bamboo skewers, soaked in water for 30 minutes

instructions: Preheat a bamboo steamer to 400°F over boiling water.
In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, garlic, ginger, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and mix until coated.
Thread 4-6 pieces of lamb, a perilla leaf, and a sprinkle of cheddar cheese onto each bamboo skewer.
Brush the skewers with olive oil and place them in the preheated bamboo steamer.
Close the steamer lid and cook for 12-15 minutes, or until the lamb is cooked to your desired level of doneness and the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with additional perilla leaves if desired.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
Serve with your favorite sides, such as roasted vegetables or a salad.
Repeat with the remaining lamb and skewers.
Discard any leftover marinade and wash your hands thoroughly after handling raw meat, as a precaution against foodborne illness.
Cover the skewers with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
